statements.statement:
// QUERY: Get step-parent relationships
MATCH (stepParent:Person)-[r:STEP_PARENT_OF]->(stepChild:Person) RETURN stepParent.name as stepParent, stepChild.name as stepChild, r.sinceDate as sinceDate

// QUERY: Get siblings
MATCH (p1:Person)-[:SIBLING_OF]-(p2:Person) RETURN p1.name as sibling1, p2.name as sibling2

// QUERY: Get deceased persons
MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.deathDate IS NOT NULL RETURN p.name, p.birthDate, p.deathDate, p.addressCity

// QUERY: Get single parents
MATCH (parent:Person)-[:PARENT_OF]->(child:Person) WITH child, collect(parent) as parents WHERE size(parents) = 1 RETURN child.name as childWithSingleParent, parents[0].name as singleParent, parents[0].gender as parentGender

// QUERY: Get polygamy families
MATCH (husband:Person)-[r:MARRIED_TO {isCurrent: true}]->(wife:Person) WITH husband, collect({wife: wife.name, order: r.order, marriedSince: r.startDate}) as wives WHERE size(wives) > 1 RETURN husband.name as polygamousHusband, [w IN wives | w.wife + ' (order: ' + toString(w.order) + ')'] as wives

// QUERY: Get divorce cases
MATCH (p1:Person)-[r:MARRIED_TO {endReason: 'divorce'}]-(p2:Person) RETURN p1.name as exSpouse1, p2.name as exSpouse2, r.startDate as marriedDate, r.endDate as divorcedDate

// QUERY: Get complete family tree
MATCH path = (head:Person)-[:PARENT_OF*0..2]->(descendant) WHERE head.name = 'Budi Santoso' RETURN [node IN nodes(path) | node.name + ' (' + node.title + ')'] as familyTree

// STATS: Count all relationships by type
MATCH ()-[r]->() RETURN type(r) as relationshipType, count(r) as count ORDER BY count DESC

// STATS: Count persons by city
MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.isDeleted = false RETURN p.addressCity as city, count(p) as personCount ORDER BY personCount DESC

// STATS: Count persons by generation
MATCH (head:Person {name: 'Budi Santoso'})-[:PARENT_OF*0..2]->(descendant:Person) WITH size((head)-[:PARENT_OF*]->(descendant)) as gen RETURN CASE gen WHEN 0 THEN 'Gen 1 (Head)' WHEN 1 THEN 'Gen 2 (Children)' WHEN 2 THEN 'Gen 3 (Grandchildren)' END as generation, count(descendant) as count ORDER BY gen
